attribute_labels.Date: Start and end date of the project
attribute_labels.alternateIdentifier: Alternative local or global identifiers for the project or activity associated with the project
attribute_labels.alternateUrl: A link to another website related to the project or activity
attribute_labels.contributor: Contributors to the project and associated properties
attribute_labels.description: Description of the project and associated properties
attribute_labels.keywords: An array of ; separated keywords describing the project
attribute_labels.organisation: Organisations associated to the project and associated properties
attribute_labels.relatedProject: Related projects and qualifying the relationship
attribute_labels.title: Title of the project and associated properties

meta.description: A project description schema based on RAiD metadata schema.
meta.name: ContextHub_project_catalogue_record

contributor.position.id.Consultant, e.g., someone hired as a con: Consultant, e.g., someone hired as a contract researcher by the project
contributor.position.id.Other Participant not covered by one of: Other Participant not covered by one of the positions above, e.g., ‘member’ or ‘other significant contributor’
contributor.position.id.Partner Investigator, e.g., industry, go: Partner Investigator, e.g., industry, government,, or community collaborator

contributor.position.id: Consultant, e.g., someone hired as a contract researcher by the project
Other Participant not covered by one of the positions above, e.g., ‘member’ or ‘other significant contributor’
Partner Investigator, e.g., industry, government, or community collaborator

attribute_information.contributor.contact: More than one contributor can be flagged as a contact.
attribute_information.contributor.family.name: The family name is in many cases is inherited and held in common by members of a family.
attribute_information.contributor.given.names: A given name, also called a first name or forename, is the part of a person’s full name that distinguishes them individually. It may be accompanied by one or more middle names and helps differentiate that person from others in a group, such as a family, who share the same last name.
attribute_information.contributor.leader: More than one contributor can be flagged as a leader if the project is jointly led.
attribute_information.contributor.position.id: contributors must have one and only one position at any given time (contributors may also be flagged as a ‘leader’ or ‘contact’ separately)
attribute_information.contributor.role.id: Changes to roles are tracked through version history rather than explicitly declared.

attribute_labels.contributor.contact: Flag indicating that the contributor is a project contact.
attribute_labels.contributor.family.name: The family name of the contributor (person) associated with the project.
attribute_labels.contributor.given.names: The given name(s) of the contributor (person) associated with the project.
attribute_labels.contributor.id: The contributor (person) associated with a project or activity identified by their ORCiD identifier
attribute_labels.contributor.leader: Flag indicating that the contributor is a project leader.
attribute_labels.contributor.position.endDate: The date the contributor terminated position associated with the project or activity
attribute_labels.contributor.position.id: A contributor’s administrative position on a project or activity
attribute_labels.contributor.position.startDate: The date the contributor began position associated with the project or activity
attribute_labels.contributor.role.id: Describing a contributor’s scientific or scholarly role on a project using the CRediT vocabulary

description.type.id.Acknowledgements: Acknowledgements, i.e., for recognition of people not listed as Contributors or organisations not listed as Organisations
description.type.id.Alternative: Alternative, i.e., an additional or supplementary full description or abstract
description.type.id.Brief: Brief, i.e., a shorted version of the primary description
description.type.id.Other: Other, i.e., any other descriptive information such as a note
description.type.id.Primary: Primary, i.e., a preferred full description or abstract

attribute_labels.description.language.id: The language used for the description text.
attribute_labels.description.text: A project description that may include any additional information not captured by other metadata properties.

attribute_information.identifier.id: This is not entered by the user but calculated when the record is created.

title.type.Alternative: Alternative, including subtitle or other supplemental title
title.type.Primary: Primary, i.e., a preferred full or long title

attribute_labels.title.endDate: The date the project or activity title was changed or stopped being used
attribute_labels.title.startDate: The date the project or activity’s title began being used
attribute_labels.title.text: A name or title by which the project or activity is known

attribute_information.date.endDate: Only year is required, month and day are optional (but recommended when available). An end date should be supplied when a project concludes.
attribute_information.date.startDate: Only the year is required, month and day are optional (but recommended when available).

attribute_labels.date.endDate: The project or activity’s end date
attribute_labels.date.startDate: The project or activity’s start date

organisation.role.id.Contractor Organisation: Contractor; A consulting organisation hired by the project
organisation.role.id.Facility Organisation: Facility; An organisation providing access to physical or digital infrastructure but not otherwise listed as a research organisation/partner organisation or contractor
organisation.role.id.Funder Organisation: Funder; An organisation underwriting the research via a cash or in-kind grant/prize/investment but not otherwise listed as a research organisation/partner organisation or contractor
organisation.role.id.Other Organisation: Other Organisation; Not covered by the roles above
organisation.role.id.Partner Organisation: Partner Organisation; A non-research organisation such as an industry/government/community partner collaborating on the project or activity as a research partner rather than a hired consultant or contractor

attribute_information.organisation.role.id: An organisation’s role may change over time, but each organisation may have one and only one role at any given time.

attribute_labels.organisation.id: The ROR identifying an organisation associated with a project or activity.
attribute_labels.organisation.name: The name of an organisation associated with a project or activity.
attribute_labels.organisation.role.endDate: The date that the organisation terminated a role associated with the project or activity
attribute_labels.organisation.role.id: Describing an organisation’s role on a project
attribute_labels.organisation.role.startDate: The date that the organisation began a role associated with the project or activity

attribute_labels.relatedProject.type.id: A description of the relationship between the activity being registered and the related resource

attribute_information.alternateIdentifier.id: an identifier applied to the project or activity
attribute_information.alternateIdentifier.type: free text description of the type of alternate identifier supplied

attribute_labels.alternateIdentifier.id: An alternate identifier of the project
attribute_labels.alternateIdentifier.type: The type of the alternate identifier of the project

attribute_information.alternateUrl.url: A link to another website related to the project or activity

attribute_labels.alternateUrl.url: A link to another webpage related to the project.
